author: abcoathup
content:
Headliner process includes selecting an upgrade focus before converging towards headliner(s)

https://ethereum-magicians.org/t/community-consensus-fork-headliners-acd-working-groups/24088#p-58740-defining-the-fork-focus-4

author: abcoathup
content: Hegotá upgrade focus looks like it will be a combination of censorship resistance & account abstraction

author: greystroke
content: I really hope then that I* will be the fork that has as its upgrade focus L1 execution scaling that has been long been promised now. Specificly, at least starting the process of actually providing shorter slot times that users have been clamoring for and promised as a near term priority. As it is, the soonest that will now happen is late 2027.

author: nero_eth
content:
L1 scaling has been the theme for Glamsterdam.
Both ePBS and BALs have a strong clear focus on scaling. The same applies to all the repricing EIPs.
I'm not saying we shouldn't scale more in I*, I'd even support that, but reading your response makes it sound like you're ignoring Glamsterdam.

I'm for shorter slots (see more details on the proposal [here](https://ethereum-magicians.org/t/the-case-for-quick-slots-in-hegota/27708)) in Hegota as a non-headliner, but we should be clear that **shorter slots is not a scaling EIP**: reducing slot time implies to reduce the gas limit and blob count in tandem - keeping throughput per time the same before and after the fork.

But let's not derail this discussion where <@703301497194152108> is saying the headliner process must focus on a general theme first, and only then pick the headliner EIP based on the theme selected.
Since this is already the case, at least based on the formal process, we have not gotten to doing a proper theme selection for Hegota, at least on the EL side. E.g. FOCIL was selected without talking about CR as a focus first and FrameTx in specific hasn't been around for long enough). With FOCIL as an example, this might have been fine because there was a strong push for it early on, swapping over from Glamsterdam. So the theme followed the concrete proposal.
On the EL side, there wasn't a clear theme so far and only now we're realizing that it should be AA.

author: wolovim
content:
---

before headliner proposals began, space was made for anyone to voice a preference for broader focus areas. wasnt a priority to reach consensus on anything - just a chance to communicate.

native AA was on the strawmap, but in this case, didnt gain momentum in ACD until 8141 offered something more concrete to talk about. maybe that changes in future forks, depending on how much consensus exists around the strawmap, and broader categories can be agreed upon without more specifics - TBD. (at least thats how i remember the series of events.)
